description: Lock the 2026-06-27 pilgrim_badge_casting correction to late-medieval tin-lead or lead-alloy cast pilgrimage souvenirs, not guild certification seals, bronze-specific casting, precious-metal badges, or generic livery badges.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to a thirteenth-century graph date within the late twelfth- through sixteenth-century source window.
Pilgrimage shrines and route economies supplied the devotional souvenir market for badges and ampullae.
The corrected material scope is cheap tin-lead or lead-alloy casting.
Series production of badges and ampullae depended on cast metal objects and molds, not bronze-only mold technology.
Guild certification seals are a regulated-authentication practice and should not be restored for shrine souvenir badge casting without a new seal-specific scope.
Bronze casting molds are too material-specific for the corrected tin-lead or lead-alloy pilgrim badge scope.
